meta.description: Emission factors used for GHG calculations in Breathe ESG
meta.notes: All factors are kg CO2e per unit unless stated otherwise. CO2e includes CO2, CH4, and N2O weighted by AR5 GWP values (CH4=28, N2O=265).

diesel.source: EPA GHG Emission Factors Hub 2025, Table 1 (Stationary Combustion); DEFRA 2024 Fuels worksheet
diesel.notes: EPA provides 10.21 kg CO2/gal for diesel. DEFRA 2024 Condensed Set reports ~2.70 kg CO2e/L for 100% mineral diesel. CH4 and N2O contributions are small (~0.07 kg CO2e/gal combined). Factor includes combustion emissions only (tank-to-wheel), not upstream/well-to-tank.

natural_gas.source: EPA GHG Emission Factors Hub 2025, Table 1 (Stationary Combustion); heat content from EPA default (1026 BTU/scf)
natural_gas.notes: EPA gives 53.06 kg CO2/MMBtu. Converted to per-m3 using: 1 m3 = 35.3147 scf × 0.001026 MMBtu/scf = 0.03623 MMBtu/m3. So 53.06 × 0.03623 = 1.922 ≈ 1.93 kg CO2e/m3. CH4 = 1.0 g/MMBtu, N2O = 0.1 g/MMBtu.

lpg.source: EPA GHG Emission Factors Hub 2025 (propane); DEFRA 2024 Fuels worksheet (LPG)
lpg.notes: EPA gives 5.68 kg CO2/gal for propane. Per liter: 5.68/3.785 = 1.50 kg CO2/L. DEFRA 2024 lists ~1.56 kg CO2e/L for LPG (which includes the slightly higher GWP-weighted CH4/N2O). Per-kg factor: ~2.99 kg CO2e/kg at LPG density of ~0.52 kg/L.

us_national_average.source: EPA eGRID2023 (released January 2025) — US national average output emission rate
us_national_average.notes: Derived from eGRID2023 national total: ~867.2 lbs CO2/MWh → 0.3937 kg CO2/kWh. This is a location-based factor. For market-based reporting, use supplier-specific or residual-mix factors. Regional eGRID subregion factors vary significantly (e.g., NWPP ≈ 0.25, SRMW ≈ 0.65 kg CO2e/kWh).

ERCT.notes: Texas grid is predominantly natural gas, wind, and solar. Lower than national average.

NWPP.notes: Heavy hydroelectric mix drives lower emission intensity.

SRMW.notes: Higher coal dependence in some Midwest states.

economy.notes: Includes radiative forcing multiplier. Domestic = flights under ~700 km (UK definition); we apply to US domestic as an approximation.

business.notes: Business class typically ~1.5-2x economy due to larger seat footprint.

economy.notes: Short haul = flights under ~3,700 km. Lower per-km than domestic due to fuel efficiency of cruise phase.
Long haul = flights over 3,700 km. Fuel-per-km drops with distance but radiative forcing applies.

business.notes: ~2.9x economy. Significant impact due to lie-flat seat area.

first.notes: ~4x economy. First class seats occupy substantially more floor space.

air_travel.source: UK Government GHG Conversion Factors 2024, Business Travel - Air tab (DESNZ/DEFRA)
air_travel.notes: Factors include radiative forcing (RF) multiplier of ~1.9x to account for non-CO2 effects of aviation (contrails, NOx, water vapour at altitude). Without RF, values are roughly half. DEFRA categorisation by haul length; we classify based on great-circle distance.

hotel_stays.source: UK Government GHG Conversion Factors 2024, Hotel Stay tab (derived from Cornell Hotel Sustainability Benchmarking Index)
hotel_stays.notes: Country-specific factors based on average hotel energy intensity. Actual emissions vary significantly by hotel class, climate zone, and energy source. These are spend-independent proxies — use actual hotel energy data when available.

car_rental.source: UK Government GHG Conversion Factors 2024, Business Travel - Land tab (average car, unknown fuel type)
car_rental.notes: Average across petrol/diesel/hybrid for a medium-sized car. If fuel type and vehicle size are known, more specific factors should be used. Excludes upstream fuel production emissions.

rail.source: UK Government GHG Conversion Factors 2024, Business Travel - Land tab (national rail, average)
rail.notes: UK national rail average. Rail emission factors vary hugely by country electrification level. Japanese Shinkansen would be lower (~0.02 kg CO2e/pkm).

unit_conversions.notes: LPG density varies by temperature; 1.9231 L/kg assumes liquid propane at 15°C (density ~0.52 kg/L).
